title: pelargonium × antonianum
intro: Pelargonium × antonianum, also known as the Cape Geranium or Barberton Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the Pelargonium genus, which is native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of rosmarinic acid. The health benefits associated with Pelargonium × antonianum include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis. Pelargonium × antonianum can be prepared for herbal use through teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves in hot water or mixing them with a solvent like ethanol.
